description_long: This is a detailed stone sculpture of the Hindu deity Ganesha, depicted with an elephant head, ornate crown, and multiple arms holding symbolic objects. The figure is seated on a square pedestal, adorned with intricate carvings and jewelry, including armlets, anklets, and a decorated sash. The trunk is gracefully curved, and the facial features are finely chiseled, with expressive eyes and a serene expression. The back of the sculpture shows the elaborate headdress and the rounded forms of the deity's body, with additional ornamental details. The surface has a matte, slightly rough finish typical of carved stone, and the overall form is solid and robust. This sculpture would be a prominent feature in a public park, temple courtyard, or urban plaza.
description: A finely carved stone sculpture of Ganesha, seated with ornate details and a serene expression, featuring an elephant head and multiple arms. The figure is richly adorned and rests on a square pedestal.
description_view_0: Side view showing Ganesha's profile, with the trunk curled and detailed arm ornaments visible.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the elephant head, crown, trunk, and symmetrical arm positioning, with full facial features and chest details.
description_view_2: Opposite side profile, displaying the other side of the trunk, arm, and decorative elements on the sash and jewelry.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the headdress, rounded shoulders, and the back of the pedestal, with less ornamental detail.
attribute_location_context: No specific context is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
